steps.title: Set w(r) equal to zero
steps.content:
When $w(r) = 0$, we have:
$$\frac{1}{r-9} - \frac{r-6}{-r+5.25} = 0$$
Move the second fraction to the right side:
$$\frac{1}{r-9} = \frac{r-6}{-r+5.25}$$

steps.title: Simplify the right side and cross-multiply
Expand and form a quadratic equation
steps.content:
Expand $(r-6)(r-9) = r^2 - 15r + 54$:
$$r - 5.25 = -r^2 + 15r - 54$$
Rearrange to standard form:
$$r^2 - 14r + 48.75 = 0$$
Multiply by 4 to clear the decimal:
$$4r^2 - 56r + 195 = 0$$

steps.title: Complete the square
steps.content:
Use $r^2-14r+48.75=0$ before the decimal-clearing step:
$$r^2-14r=-48.75$$
$$r^2-14r+49=0.25$$
$$(r-7)^2=0.25$$
So $r=7\pm0.5$, giving $r=6.5$ or $r=7.5$. The greatest possible value is 7.5, or 15/2.
